description_long: This is a roughly hand-formed ceramic bowl with a wide, open mouth and a slightly flared rim. The surface is matte and uneven, with visible textural irregularities and a patchy coloration that transitions from a light beige to a reddish-brown. One section of the rim and upper body is covered in a lighter, almost white slip or restoration material, contrasting with the rest of the bowl's earthy tones. The bowl's form is somewhat asymmetrical, with a gently bulging midsection and a base that is narrower than the rim. There are visible labels or inventory tags affixed to the exterior, suggesting it may be an archaeological or museum piece. The interior appears empty and similarly textured to the exterior, with no visible glaze.
description: A hand-formed, asymmetrical ceramic bowl with a wide mouth, matte surface, and patchy beige to reddish-brown coloration, featuring a lighter restored section and visible inventory labels.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from an angle highlighting the uneven rim and a large, light-colored patch covering part of the upper exterior.
description_view_1: This view shows the lighter patch more prominently, along with a visible inventory label on the inner rim.
description_view_2: The bowl's mouth appears more circular, with the light patch and label visible on the far side, and the reddish-brown body more apparent.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the bowl, showing a more uniform reddish-brown surface and a rectangular label on the exterior, with less of the light patch visible.
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, possibly for historical or display purposes

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
